advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh is also refreshed on a probe-only change so it matches the installed /usr/local/bin copy (rsync or install from DEPLOY_REPO).
evidence: autodeploy.sh:207 `elif ! bash "$MIRROR_DIR/infra/scripts/install-observability.sh"; then` installs from the mirror checkout
install-observability.sh:12 `install -m 0755 "${SCRIPT_DIR}/observability.sh" /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh` copies relative to its own dir (the mirror), not from $DEPLOY_REPO
rsync into $DEPLOY_REPO only runs inside the BOT_PATHS block (autodeploy.sh:161), which does NOT fire for a probe-only change since OBS paths are absent from BOT_PATHS (autodeploy.sh:55)
ticket AC: '/usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh matches /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh after an autodeploy run'
advisory.finding: On a probe-ONLY change the installed /usr/local/bin copy is refreshed from the MIRROR but /opt/rebar (DEPLOY_REPO) is never rsynced, so /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh and /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh diverge — violating the ticket's acceptance criterion that they match post-deploy.
scenarios: A commit changing only infra/scripts/observability.sh lands on main; autodeploy refreshes /usr/local/bin but /opt/rebar stays at the prior version.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a drift/regression check tying the installed-probe path to a single source of truth (AC#2) and cover the obs-materialize-failure err-marker branch.
evidence: The single test asserts only `marker.exists()` (test file lines ~101-104)

ticket AC: 'A drift check (config-check or a test) fails when the materialised copy can go stale — i.e. the install path is derived from one source of truth, mirroring the b005 --print-volumes pattern'
ABSENCE: no test drives the obs-materialize-failed branch (autodeploy.sh:206/208) to assert the err marker + non-fatal continuation
Only one test exists: `def test_autodeploy_rematerializes_probe_on_source_change`

git stub reports a change ONLY for observability.sh: `case "$*" in *observability.sh*) echo "infra/scripts/observability.sh"; exit 0 ;; esac` — the no-change path is never asserted against the installer marker
autodeploy.sh:203 `if changed "$OBS_PATHS"; then` — the guard whose regression (widened glob) is untested
ABSENCE: no test drives a diff of an unrelated path and asserts `marker.exists()` is False
advisory.finding: The test only proves the installer re-runs on a probe change; it does not cover the ticket's AC#2 drift check that fails when the materialized copy can go stale (single-source-of-truth path derivation), nor the failure/err-marker path.
merged_from: No test asserts the negative case: when a changed set contains NO OBS_PATHS entry, autodeploy must NOT re-run install-observability.sh. The sole test only exercises the observability.sh-changed branch, so a regression that widened the OBS_PATHS glob (or made the trigger unconditional) — re-running the installer on every deploy — would pass the suite.
scenarios: A future edit changes install-observability.sh's copy target or drops the OBS trigger; no drift/regression test would catch it.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test where install-observability.sh exits non-zero and assert (a) autodeploy still advances deployed-sha to TARGET and (b) stderr contains an `obs-materialize-failed` AUTODEPLOY_ERROR marker.
evidence: autodeploy.sh:207-208 `elif ! bash "$MIRROR_DIR/infra/scripts/install-observability.sh"; then / err obs-materialize-failed "install-observability.sh failed; ..."`
autodeploy.sh:215 `echo "$TARGET" > "$SHA_FILE.tmp" && mv "$SHA_FILE.tmp" "$SHA_FILE"` — success advances even after an OBS failure (the non-fatal claim)

Installer stub always succeeds: `installer.write_text(f'#!/usr/bin/env bash
echo ran > "{marker}"
exit 0
')` — never exercises the failure branch
ABSENCE: no assertion on `result.stderr` containing `obs-materialize-failed`, and no assertion on the deployed-sha file after an OBS failure
advisory.finding: The diff's core safety property — a probe-refresh failure is NON-FATAL and emits an `obs-materialize-failed` AUTODEPLOY_ERROR marker (so staleness is alarmed, not silent) while the deploy still succeeds — is untested. No test drives the installer-failure or checkout-failure branch, asserts the `AUTODEPLOY_ERROR ... obs-materialize-failed` marker on stderr, or asserts deployed-sha is still advanced.
advisory.suggested_fix: Parametrize the installer stub to exit 1; assert `"obs-materialize-failed" in result.stderr` and that the deployed-sha file now equals _TARGET.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Sync $MIRROR_DIR into $DEPLOY_REPO on an OBS-only change (or install the probe from $DEPLOY_REPO) so /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh matches /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh post-deploy.
evidence: autodeploy.sh:205 `if ! git -C "$MIRROR_DIR" checkout -q "$TARGET" 2>/dev/null; then`

autodeploy.sh:207 `elif ! bash "$MIRROR_DIR/infra/scripts/install-observability.sh"; then` — installer sourced from MIRROR_DIR, and no rsync into DEPLOY_REPO occurs in this block (contrast BOT_PATHS block autodeploy.sh:161 `rsync -a --delete "${RSYNC_EXCLUDES[@]}" "$MIRROR_DIR/" "$DEPLOY_REPO/"`)

ticket dying-verastile-quelea AC: 'Evidence on the box post-deploy: /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh matches /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh after an autodeploy run.' — the OBS-only path leaves /opt/rebar stale
advisory.finding: On an observability-only change, autodeploy re-runs install-observability.sh from $MIRROR_DIR but never rsyncs MIRROR_DIR into $DEPLOY_REPO, so /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh stays stale while /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh is refreshed — the two copies drift, violating the ticket's post-deploy match criterion.
scenarios: A commit changes only infra/scripts/observability.sh; BOT_PATHS diff is empty so the rsync block at lines 155-167 is skipped; the OBS block refreshes /usr/local/bin but /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh is never updated.
advisory.suggested_fix: Either rsync MIRROR_DIR->DEPLOY_REPO in the OBS block before running the installer, or run the installer from $DEPLOY_REPO/infra/scripts/install-observability.sh after ensuring DEPLOY_REPO reflects TARGET, so both the build-context copy and the /usr/local/bin copy come from one materialized source.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Only advance deployed-sha after the DEPLOY_REPO build context has actually been materialized to TARGET (i.e. rsync on the OBS path too), so the recorded SHA never overstates /opt/rebar's contents.
evidence: autodeploy.sh:215 `echo "$TARGET" > "$SHA_FILE.tmp" && mv "$SHA_FILE.tmp" "$SHA_FILE"`
OBS block (autodeploy.sh:203-212) performs no rsync into $DEPLOY_REPO, so /opt/rebar remains at the prior tree while deployed-sha becomes TARGET
advisory.finding: deployed-sha is advanced to TARGET (line 215) even when only OBS paths changed and $DEPLOY_REPO was never rsynced to TARGET, so the recorded deployed SHA overstates the actual contents of the /opt/rebar build context.
scenarios: OBS-only change lands; deployed-sha=TARGET is written but /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh was never synced; a subsequent BOT_PATHS diff computed from this deployed-sha treats the build context as if it already reflects TARGET.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a config-check drift gate that derives OBS_PATHS/install path from one source of truth so a materialized-copy staleness cannot recur undetected.
evidence: ticket dying-verastile-quelea AC: 'A drift check (config-check or a test) fails when the materialised copy can go stale — i.e. the install path is derived from one source of truth, mirroring the b005 --print-volumes pattern.'
config-check.sh header lists checks 1-5 (config-check.sh:12-21) with no observability install-path drift gate
test file test_autodeploy_materialize_probe.py only asserts the installer re-runs when the probe changed (happy path), not that the two copies stay derived from one source
advisory.finding: The change adds a runtime re-materialize and a happy-path test but no single-source-of-truth drift gate (config-check) that fails when the installed probe copy can silently go stale, which the ticket explicitly requires (AC #2).
scenarios: A future refactor renames observability.sh or splits it so OBS_PATHS no longer matches every installer-materialized artifact; the trigger silently misses it and the /usr/local/bin copy goes stale again — the exact recurrence class of this bug.

coaching.coaching: File a follow-on ticket (discovered_from this change) for the unsynced /opt/rebar probe copy on probe-only changes, a pre-existing / out-of-scope issue — do not fix it in this change.
coaching.subject: the unsynced /opt/rebar probe copy on probe-only changes

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the non-fatal probe-refresh failure and err marker.
coaching.subject: the non-fatal probe-refresh failure and err marker

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the single-source-of-truth probe drift gate.
